explanation: The LTL property encodes that once the self-test has passed (selfTestPass) and the configuration loading and internal checking are complete (initDone), then it is guaranteed from then on that the system will both start monitoring (startMonitoring) and reporting (startReportingHealthParams) health parameters. This captures the requirement that these operational steps only begin after the proper initialization and self-test procedures have finished successfully.

The property expresses that once both the self-test has passed (selfTestPass) and the configurations have been loaded (initDone), the system will always start monitoring and reporting health parameters (startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams). The implication is universal: after self-test and configuration load, both monitoring and reporting commence, matching the requirement about system readiness after self-test and configuration.

The requirement is that after (once) the self-test passes and the configurations/initialization are loaded, the system should start monitoring and reporting health parameters. 'selfTestPass' indicates the self-test completed successfully. 'initDone' indicates the configurations are loaded and internal checking is terminated. The requirement is: If both selfTestPass and initDone are true, then eventually startReportingHealthParams and startMonitoring should become true. Since reporting and monitoring health parameters are closely related in this context, we can combine them with an AND. We choose Template 44: 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))', where bool_exp1 = selfTestPass, bool_exp2 = initDone, and bool_exp3 = (startReportingHealthParams & startMonitoring). N_DURATION is set to 10 as a reasonable fixed integer.

The requirement states that after successful self-test and completion of configuration loading, the system must start monitoring and reporting health parameters. In the atomic proposition list, 'selfTestPass' represents successful completion of the self-test, and 'initDone' represents configurations being loaded successfully. The action to start monitoring and reporting is captured by 'startReportingHealthParams', and 'startMonitoring' (if needed). The correct LTL template is: G((selfTestPass & initDone) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] startReportingHealthParams)), which expresses that whenever both the self-test has passed and initialization is done, within a fixed time window, the system must start reporting health parameters. This directly formalizes the step-wise activation sequence required in the natural language statement.

The requirement states that the system must start monitoring and reporting health parameters only after the self-test has passed and configurations have been properly loaded. This means that 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' must both be true, and only then should 'startMonitoring' and 'startReportingHealthParams' occur. The best template is an implication that globally, if the preconditions were satisfied, then the health monitoring and reporting start. This is captured using template 40: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 is 'selfTestPass', bool_exp2 is 'initDone', and bool_exp3 is '(startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams)'. This ensures that throughout the system operation, health monitoring and reporting will only initiate after successful self-test and configuration loading.

The property specifies that after both the self-test has passed and the system configurations have loaded successfully (i.e., selfTestPass and initDone are both true), the system will always start monitoring and reporting health parameters (i.e., startMonitoring and startReportingHealthParams both become true and remain so). This captures the natural language requirement that the system only begins monitoring and reporting health parameters after successful self-test and configuration, and this will be maintained henceforth. The template used enforces this as a global (always) implication: if the conditions are both satisfied, then the required actions always follow.

The requirement states that after both the self-test and configuration loading are completed successfully, the system should start monitoring and reporting health parameters. Among the atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' indicates a successful self-test, 'initDone' denotes that configurations are loaded, 'startMonitoring' is for starting to monitor, and 'startReportingHealthParams' signals start of reporting health parameters.

We want to express that whenever both 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' become true (meaning system is ready), the system will (in the next state) start monitoring and reporting health parameters. This is a classic cause/effect dependency: "IF (both self-test passes and configurations loaded), THEN (in the next state) start monitoring AND reporting health parameters."

We use template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), so that 'bool_exp1' = selfTestPass, 'bool_exp2' = initDone, and 'bool_exp3' = startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams. This guarantees that every time both preconditions are met, in the next step, the two specified actions will occur.

The requirement states that after both a successful self-test AND successful configuration loading, the system should begin monitoring and reporting health parameters. The atomic propositions 'selfTestPass' (indicates all self-test steps passed successfully), 'initDone' (indicates the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated), 'startMonitoring' (indicates system start monitoring health parameters), and 'startReportingHealthParams' (indicates start reporting health parameters) are relevant. We want that whenever both 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' have occurred, it must eventually be the case that both 'startMonitoring' AND 'startReportingHealthParams' become true at some point (since they might occur in the future). The selected template is 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))' (id 44), which captures that globally, after the success conditions, the required actions eventually happen. Here, bool_exp1 = 'selfTestPass', bool_exp2 = 'initDone', bool_exp3 = '(startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams)', no bool_exp4 needed. N_DURATION can be set (for example) to 10 to ensure a finite, reasonable delay is allowed after the initialization steps for the monitoring/reporting to start.

The natural language requirement states that the system should start monitoring and reporting health parameters, but only after the self-test and configuration loading are both completed successfully. In the atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' means the self-test has completed successfully and 'initDone' indicates configurations were loaded and checks were complete. The desired property is that whenever both 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' are true (i.e., both the self-test and configs are done), then eventually the system will start monitoring ('startMonitoring') and reporting health parameters ('startReportingHealthParams'). Since both monitoring and reporting must start as a result, we encode the implication of both conditions jointly leading to the conjunction of the two actions. The most direct way to capture this is with a "globally, if antecedent then eventually consequence" template, with the antecedent being the conjunction of 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone', and the consequence is the conjunction of 'startMonitoring' and 'startReportingHealthParams', within a reasonable time window N_DURATION.

The input states that after the self-test passes and the configurations are loaded successfully, the system must begin monitoring and reporting health parameters. This requirement can be phrased as: Whenever selfTestPass (self-test passes) and initDone (configurations loaded successfully) become true, it should eventually follow (within a bounded duration) that both monitoring (startMonitoring) and reporting (startReportingHealthParams) start. The LTL template that matches this cause-effect structure is template 44: 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))'.

In this context:
- bool_exp1 = selfTestPass (self-test passes)
- bool_exp2 = initDone (configurations loaded successfully)
- bool_exp3 = (startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams) (system starts monitoring AND reporting health)

This property expresses that globally, whenever both self-test and initialization succeed, the system must eventually (within N_DURATION steps) start both monitoring and reporting health parameters.
